pages.raw_text:
Almost the staff of this conservation business was the
withdrawal from entry by Roosevelt of lands affordng
water power


And Roosevelt right Water power is far more
precious than gold diamonds. When shall have
learned that gold is dross and diamonds worthless
as quartz these water that Roosevelt tried to save
from the clutch of the great electric companies will be fur
nshng power, heat and light to the Every of
them WILL year after year. century after century, aeon
after be doing work making earth easier and bet
ter place in which to live-for some one.


For whom'


Roosevelt wanted them work throughout all time for
the people whole.
\ Will they?


The answer to be up to Mr. Taft If So he will
meet momentous problem in his administration.
He will dispose of the welfare of millions of and
women unborn, when he decides In the name of a
better America may he have wisdom and patriotism to
decide Alright


When Garfield secretary of the interior he had lists
made by the reclamation service engineers of all these
water power Sites and fast the lists turned in
Roosevelt withdrew them from entry Some of them were
homestead lands, mineral lands desert lands,
some timber and stone lands. All of them being
eagerly snapped up not for homes, but for monopoly
through all ages.


Mr. BalIinger has been said to be opposed to the whole
conservation movement. He is from the part of the
try where the business of land grabbing looked upon by
many the which will ''develop' the country He
may be blind with the blindness of that spirit of short
sighted greed. He may not


But anyhow he is restoring the water powers, and coal
lands too, to the list of lands open to entryand open
to loot.


Whatever may be Mr. Ballngers mental attitude he is
making possible atrocous robbery of the people Of this
nation and not on of the people of today but their chill
dren and not only their children but their posterty liter
ally long the shines and the water 1t is the
height the depth, the acme of iniquty.


Mr.. Taft, stop him!


Save the water powers and the coal and the phosphaes
for the people Remember what you have said about the
Roosevelt policies. Nobody hold you to literal
performance of all RooseveIs policies But those in the
inception of which Roosevelt to such high statesman
ship-camy them out!


Mr.. Banger says he knows of no law for holding these
precious lands from entry Roosevets secretary and his
lawyers satisfied And in the name of justice stop
Ballnger until the court of last resort says you must let
them be entered.
